figures.name: Tertullian
figures.title: The Father of Latin Theology
figures.quote: The blood of the martyrs is the seed of the Church.
figures.summary: Brilliant Carthaginian theologian who coined terms like 'Trinity' and forcefully defended Christianity against pagan critics.

figures.name: Perpetua & Felicitas
figures.title: Martyrs of Carthage
figures.quote: Stand fast in the faith, and love one another, all of you...
figures.summary: A noblewoman and her enslaved servant who stood together in faith and faced martyrdom in the arena, breaking down Roman social barriers.

figures.name: Cyprian of Carthage
figures.title: Bishop and Martyr
figures.quote: He cannot have God as Father who does not have the Church as mother.
figures.summary: Bishop who navigated the lapsi crisis with pastoral care, championed church unity, and was ultimately martyred.

figures.name: Augustine of Hippo
figures.title: Doctor of Grace
figures.quote: You have made us for yourself, O Lord, and our hearts are restless until they rest in you.
figures.summary: The most influential Christian thinker of the first millennium; his writings on grace, sin, and the City of God shaped Western theology.
